hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Drives YAP/TEAD-Dependent Super-Enhancer Assembly via EP300/BRD4 Co-Recruitment at Mechanosensitive Cell-Identity Loci
hypotheses.core_claim: Tumor-relevant ECM stiffening (2-20 kPa) triggers YAP nuclear translocation, nucleating EP300 and BRD4 co-recruitment to mechanosensitive super-enhancers controlling cell-identity genes. This produces a stiffness-dose-dependent H3K27ac super-enhancer gain signature with TEAD motif enrichment, qualitatively different from shear-stress-driven KLF/ETS enhancer changes.
mechanism_steps: ECM stiffness increase (1-25 kPa range) activates integrin clustering and RhoA-ROCK1-actomyosin contractility
ROCK1-MYH9 actomyosin forces flatten nucleus (10-30% volume change; passive LINC insufficient)
LATS1/2 inactivation allows YAP dephosphorylation and nuclear translocation
Nuclear YAP binds TEAD1-4 at CATTCC motif enhancer elements
YAP/TEAD recruits EP300 acetyltransferase (STRING 0.692) which deposits H3K27ac
H3K27ac recruits BRD4 (STRING YAP1-BRD4 0.691) to form super-enhancer condensates
TEAD-motif enriched super-enhancers activated at MYC/SOX9/TWIST1/CTGF loci
hypotheses.key_bridge: YAP/TEAD nuclear translocation recruits EP300/BRD4 to deposit H3K27ac at super-enhancers (enzymatic/signaling bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: H3K27ac ChIP-seq on MCF10A cells at 1-25 kPa PAA gels: stiffness-gained peaks enriched for TEAD motifs; super-enhancers increase at 10-25 kPa; verteporfin abolishes TEAD-motif enhancer gains; motif profile differs from Tsaryk 2022 shear-stress KLF pattern
hypotheses.counter_evidence: YAP may recruit Mediator directly bypassing H3K27ac; 14 YAP+super-enhancer papers may partially cover this; stiffness may be permissive not instructive; MCF10A may lack sufficient YAP target super-enhancers
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: PubMed: matrix stiffness + H3K27ac = 1 paper. No H3K27ac ChIP-seq under ECM stiffness gradients exists. Stiffness vs. shear stress enhancer program distinction (TEAD vs. KLF motifs) completely novel.

hypotheses.title: LMNA-Dependent Mechanical Tension Repositions CTCF-Anchored TAD Boundaries, Creating Stiffness-Specific Enhancer-Promoter Loop Topologies
hypotheses.core_claim: ECM stiffness forces transmitted through LINC complex to nuclear lamina physically displace lamin A/C-associated CTCF boundary elements, shifting TAD boundaries by 50-200 kb and creating novel enhancer-promoter contacts impossible in soft-ECM TAD configuration.
mechanism_steps: ECM stiffness upregulates lamin A/C expression and increases nuclear lamina tension via LINC complex (LMNA-EMD 0.999, LMNA-SUN2 0.999)
ROCK1-actomyosin forces (120-920 pN at nucleus) stretch the nuclear lamina
Lamina-anchored CTCF sites (at LAD-proximal TAD boundaries) are physically displaced
CTCF boundary insulation weakened at displaced sites (LMNA-CTCF STRING 0.654)
Cohesin extrudes past weakened boundaries, forming new inter-TAD enhancer-promoter loops
Non-LAD TAD boundaries remain unaffected (internal control)
hypotheses.key_bridge: LMNA-CTCF mechanical coupling transmits ECM forces to TAD boundary positions (structural/mechanical bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: Hi-C on MSCs at 1 vs 25 kPa: >=5% TAD boundaries shift at LAD-proximal regions; new inter-TAD contacts emerge at shifted boundaries; siLMNA partially restores soft-ECM topology; Y-27632 abolishes shifts
hypotheses.counter_evidence: CTCF binding dominated by DNA sequence not force; STRING 0.654 may be indirect; TAD boundaries are highly conserved across cell types; passive LINC gives <0.3% strain, even active forces may dissipate before reaching specific CTCF sites
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: Zero Hi-C experiments under ECM stiffness conditions. LMNA-CTCF mechanical coupling as TAD boundary modulator is novel. LAD-proximal boundary sensitivity prediction provides falsifiable framework.

hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness Coordinates KDM6B-EP300 Enzymatic Handoff to Resolve Bivalent Enhancers into Active States During Mechanically-Gated Cell Fate Transitions
hypotheses.core_claim: ECM stiffness simultaneously upregulates KDM6B (H3K27me3 demethylase) and activates EP300 (H3K27ac acetyltransferase) via parallel mechanotransduction pathways, creating a coordinated enzymatic handoff at bivalent enhancers: KDM6B removes H3K27me3, then EP300 deposits H3K27ac on the same K27 residue, converting poised bivalent enhancers to active enhancers in stiffness-dependent manner.
mechanism_steps: ECM stiffness (1-30 kPa) upregulates KDM6B expression and activity (KDM6B 2025 paper)
KDM6B removes H3K27me3 at bivalent enhancers carrying H3K27me3 + H3K4me1
H3K27 residue is now unmodified (methyl removed, available for acetylation)
Parallel: ECM stiffness activates YAP/TEAD which recruits EP300 (STRING 0.692)
EP300 deposits H3K27ac on the now-vacant K27 residue
Bivalent enhancer resolved to active enhancer (H3K27ac + H3K4me1)
Active enhancer drives cell fate gene transcription (EMT, osteogenesis)
hypotheses.key_bridge: KDM6B demethylation + EP300 acetylation coordinated at shared bivalent enhancer targets (dual-enzyme coordination bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: ChIP-seq for H3K27me3, H3K27ac, H3K4me1 on MCF10A at 2 vs 20 kPa: bivalent enhancers resolve to active on stiff ECM; GSK-J4 (KDM6B inhibitor) blocks both H3K27me3 removal AND H3K27ac gain; A-485 (EP300 inhibitor) allows H3K27me3 removal but blocks H3K27ac; time-course shows H3K27me3 loss precedes H3K27ac gain by >6h
hypotheses.counter_evidence: KDM6B may act at promoters not enhancers; intermediate H3K27me0 state may be too transient or re-methylated by EZH2; different stiffness thresholds for KDM6B vs YAP activation may decouple the handoff; bivalent enhancers may not exist at mechanosensitive loci in all cell types
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: Literature treats KDM6B H3K27me3 removal and EP300 H3K27ac deposition as separate mechanically-regulated events. No paper integrates them as coordinated handoff at shared bivalent enhancer targets. Gap analysis anomaly #3 explicitly identifies this coordination as unstudied.

hypotheses.title: PIEZO1-Gated Calcium Oscillations Activate CaMKII-Dependent DOT1L Stabilization, Depositing H3K79me2 at Gene Bodies to License Stiffness-Responsive Transcriptional Elongation
hypotheses.core_claim: On stiff ECM, PIEZO1 mechanosensitive channel opening generates calcium oscillations activating CaMKII, which phosphorylates and stabilizes DOT1L. DOT1L deposits H3K79me2 at gene bodies adjacent to stiffness-activated enhancers, creating a two-key model: enhancer H3K27ac provides the address, gene body H3K79me2 provides the elongation license.
mechanism_steps: ECM stiffness opens PIEZO1 mechanosensitive Ca2+ channel (sustained membrane tension)
Repetitive PIEZO1 opening generates Ca2+ oscillations (not single spike)
Ca2+ oscillations activate CaMKII via frequency-dependent autophosphorylation at T286
CaMKII phosphorylates DOT1L, stabilizing it against proteasomal degradation (PREDICTED)
Stabilized DOT1L deposits H3K79me2 at gene bodies of mechanosensitive genes
H3K79me2 licenses transcriptional elongation through gene body (SEC recruitment)
Genes require BOTH enhancer H3K27ac (from H1) AND gene body H3K79me2 for full activation
hypotheses.key_bridge: PIEZO1 Ca2+ oscillations -> CaMKII frequency decoding -> DOT1L stabilization -> H3K79me2 at gene bodies (ion channel/calcium signaling bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: H3K79me2 ChIP-seq on MSCs at 1 vs 25 kPa: stiffness-dependent gain at gene bodies not enhancers; GsMTx4 (PIEZO1 inhibitor) abolishes H3K79me2 gain but retains H3K27ac at enhancers; KN-93 (CaMKII inhibitor) blocks H3K79me2 gain; genes with both H3K27ac enhancers and H3K79me2 gene bodies show robust mRNA vs Pol II pausing for H3K27ac-only genes
hypotheses.counter_evidence: CaMKII-DOT1L phosphorylation is unconfirmed; H3K79me2 may not be required for elongation at mechanosensitive genes; PIEZO1 inactivation kinetics (~30ms) may prevent sustained oscillations on static stiff ECM; DOT1L may be transcriptionally upregulated (not post-translationally stabilized)
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: PIEZO1-DOT1L 2025 paper identified endpoint connection but not intermediate cascade. CaMKII as bridge kinase is novel. Two-key model (enhancer mark + gene body mark) for productive mechanotransduction-driven transcription is a novel framework.

hypotheses.title: Physical Chromatin Stretching Under ECM Stiffness Increases Enhancer-Promoter Distance for Proximal Genes While Decreasing It for Distal Genes, Creating Distance-Dependent Transcriptional Reversal Within TADs
hypotheses.core_claim: Nuclear volume increase (10-30%) on stiff ECM physically stretches chromatin fibers within TADs, increasing the bending energy cost for tight enhancer-promoter loops (<50 kb) while reducing 3D distance for large-scale loops (>200 kb). This creates a paradoxical distance-dependent reversal: nearby genes are silenced while distal genes are activated by the same enhancer.
mechanism_steps: ECM stiffness drives ROCK1-actomyosin nuclear flattening (10-30% volume change)
Nuclear flattening physically stretches chromatin fibers within TADs
Stretched fiber has increased persistence length and reduced flexibility
For nearby genes (<50 kb from enhancer): tight loop formation is opposed by increased bending energy
For distal genes (>200 kb from enhancer): stretched fiber reduces 3D distance, increasing contact probability
Net effect: distance-dependent sign reversal in enhancer-promoter contact changes within TADs
Paradoxical silencing of proximal genes + activation of distal genes by same enhancer
hypotheses.key_bridge: Physical chromatin fiber stretching alters polymer physics of loop formation geometry (direct biophysical/force bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: Hi-C + RNA-seq on IMR90 at 1 vs 25 kPa: for each active enhancer, contact probability with genes <50 kb decreases on stiff ECM while contact with genes >200 kb increases; RNA-seq confirms distance-dependent expression reversal; Y-27632 abolishes the pattern; crossover distance correlates with chromatin persistence length
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Nuclear flattening may redistribute chromatin uniformly without distance-dependent geometry; cohesin loop extrusion dominates over polymer physics; 10-30% volume change may be absorbed by nuclear envelope not transmitted to chromatin; Hi-C resolution on hydrogels is technically challenging
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: No paper has applied polymer physics of chromatin loop formation to predict distance-dependent enhancer-promoter contact changes under mechanical force. Paradoxical silencing of proximal genes is a counterintuitive prediction with no precedent.

hypotheses.title: ECM Stiffness History Is Encoded as Persistent H3K27ac at Super-Enhancers via BRD4-EP300 Positive Feedback, Creating a Bistable Epigenetic Ratchet for Mechanical Memory
hypotheses.core_claim: Cells exposed to transient ECM stiffening retain H3K27ac marks at super-enhancers (but not typical enhancers) for weeks after matrix softening. This mechanical memory is maintained by a self-reinforcing BRD4-EP300 positive feedback loop that, once nucleated by YAP, persists independently of continued mechanical input -- creating a bistable switch at cell-identity super-enhancers.
mechanism_steps: ECM stiffness activates YAP/TEAD -> EP300 -> H3K27ac deposition at enhancers (from H1)
H3K27ac is read by BRD4 bromodomain; BRD4 recruits additional EP300
EP300 deposits more H3K27ac on neighboring nucleosomes (positive feedback loop)
At super-enhancers: high BRD4/MED1/EP300 density creates phase-separated condensate
Condensate is thermodynamically stable once nucleated; no longer requires YAP input
Upon matrix softening: YAP exits nucleus but super-enhancer condensate persists (bistable ON state)
Typical enhancers (low BRD4/EP300 density) cannot sustain feedback loop; revert to OFF state
Result: bimodal memory -- super-enhancers retain memory, typical enhancers do not
hypotheses.key_bridge: BRD4-EP300 positive feedback loop creates self-maintaining H3K27ac at super-enhancers after YAP departure (self-reinforcing epigenetic bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: MCF10A on 25 kPa for 72h then transferred to 1 kPa: H3K27ac ChIP-seq at 0/24/72h/7d post-softening; >80% typical enhancers lose H3K27ac by 24h but >50% super-enhancers retain at 72h; JQ1 (BRD4 inhibitor) at softening abolishes super-enhancer memory; single-cell ATAC-seq shows bimodal accessibility at super-enhancer loci post-softening
hypotheses.counter_evidence: H3K27ac turnover t1/2 ~30-90 min may prevent memory without continuous signaling; HDAC3 re-expression on soft ECM may overwhelm EP300 maintenance; DNA methylation not H3K27ac may be the true mechanical memory substrate; YAP may contribute to condensate formation making persistence YAP-dependent
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: Hsia 2023 proposed mechanical memory but not at enhancer resolution. No test of H3K27ac persistence at super-enhancers after softening. BRD4-EP300 positive feedback as molecular mechanism for bistable mechanical memory is novel.

hypotheses.title: Tissue-Native ECM Stiffness Values Specify Tissue-Specific Enhancer Programs, and Disease-Associated Stiffening Activates Ectopic Enhancers from Non-Cognate Tissue Programs
hypotheses.core_claim: Each tissue's characteristic ECM stiffness (brain ~0.5 kPa, liver ~1.5 kPa, muscle ~12 kPa, bone ~50 kPa) selects a distinct enhancer repertoire via stiffness-dose-dependent TF combinatorial codes. Disease stiffening shifts tissue stiffness into a different tissue's range, ectopically activating that non-cognate tissue's enhancer program (e.g., liver fibrosis at 15 kPa activates muscle-program enhancers).
mechanism_steps: Each tissue has characteristic ECM stiffness spanning 0.1-50 kPa range
Different stiffness ranges activate different TF combinations via graded mechanotransduction
Low stiffness (~0.5 kPa): YAP cytoplasmic; SOX2/POU-dependent neural enhancers active
Medium stiffness (~12 kPa): YAP/TEAD + MYOD/MRF activate myogenic enhancers
High stiffness (~50 kPa): full YAP/TEAD + RUNX2 activate osteogenic super-enhancers
Each stiffness-TF combination selects distinct H3K27ac enhancer repertoire
Disease stiffening (liver 1.5->15 kPa) shifts stiffness into muscle range
Non-cognate muscle-program enhancers (incl. alpha-SMA) ectopically activated in liver cells
hypotheses.key_bridge: Stiffness-dose-dependent TF combinatorial code selects tissue-specific enhancer programs (developmental gradient bridge)
hypotheses.falsifiable_prediction: iPSCs on PAA at 0.5/2/8/15/25/50 kPa for 14d without growth factors: H3K27ac ChIP-seq + RNA-seq at each stiffness; each range activates distinct enhancer program with tissue-specific TF motifs; overlap with ENCODE tissue catalogs; hepatic stellate cells on 1.5 vs 15 kPa: 15 kPa cells activate muscle/connective tissue enhancers; verteporfin blocks medium-to-high stiffness switch but not lowest
hypotheses.counter_evidence: Cell-intrinsic chromatin state dominates over ECM stiffness; tissue stiffness has substantial local heterogeneity; iPSCs without growth factors may not differentiate; disease stiffening accompanied by inflammation/hypoxia confounding stiffness effects
hypotheses.novelty_rationale: Gap analysis explicitly identifies tissue-specific enhancer programs as function of ECM stiffness as unexplored. No systematic enhancer profiling across full tissue stiffness range. Non-cognate tissue program activation in disease is a novel mechanistic framework for fibrosis and tumor reprogramming.

generation_notes: Generated 7 hypotheses with 7 distinct bridge mechanisms. All address the critical gap: ECM STIFFNESS (not shear stress) to ENHANCER landscape changes. Bridge mechanisms: (1) YAP/TEAD-EP300/BRD4 enzymatic signaling, (2) LMNA-CTCF structural/mechanical coupling, (3) KDM6B-EP300 dual-enzyme coordination, (4) PIEZO1-Ca2+/CaMKII ion channel signaling, (5) Physical chromatin stretching polymer physics, (6) BRD4-EP300 self-reinforcing epigenetic feedback, (7) Stiffness-dependent TF combinatorial developmental gradient. Self-critique completed: all generator warnings respected (PIEZO1-DOT1L indirect only, LMNA-HDAC2 not HDAC3, active ROCK1-actomyosin required, super-enhancer framed as novel). Claim-level verification completed for all 7 hypotheses.
